allow:
Bash(./create-new-feature.ps1 -Json "Build the Platform Core \(Layer 1\) for Al-Saada Smart Bot — a Telegram bot platform for Egyptian business management.

This is Feature 001 and the foundation that everything else will be built on. NO modules or Flow Blocks in this feature — only the core platform infrastructure.

## What to build:

### 1. Bot Engine Setup
- Initialize grammY bot with TypeScript
- Configure Hono server for webhook mode
- Setup Pino logger with Arabic-friendly formatting
- Environment configuration with Zod validation \(.env schema\)
- Graceful shutdown handling
- Error handling middleware \(catches all errors, logs them, sends user-friendly Arabic message\)

### 2. Docker Infrastructure
- Docker Compose with 3 services: PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7, Bot \(Node.js\)
- Prisma ORM connected to PostgreSQL
- ioredis connected to Redis
- Health check endpoints
- Volume persistence for database data

### 3. User Registration Flow
- When any user sends /start to the bot for the first time:
  - Bot checks if user exists in database \(by Telegram ID\)
  - If NEW user: Show welcome message in Arabic, ask them to submit a join request \(name + phone number\)
  - Save join request to database with status ''PENDING''
  - Notify all Super Admins about the new join request
  - User sees ''Your request is pending approval'' message
  - If EXISTING user with VISITOR role \(pending\): Show ''still waiting for approval'' message
  - If EXISTING user with EMPLOYEE role: Show employee menu
  - If EXISTING user with ADMIN role: Show admin menu
  - If EXISTING user with SUPER_ADMIN role: Show super admin menu

### 4. RBAC System \(Role-Based Access Control\)
- 4 roles stored in database: SUPER_ADMIN, ADMIN, EMPLOYEE, VISITOR
- Middleware that checks user role before EVERY action
- Super Admin: sees everything, can do everything
- Admin: sees only their assigned sections/modules \(scope stored in database as JSON array of section IDs and module IDs\)
- Employee: sees only their own data and can submit requests
- Visitor: can only see welcome screen and submit join request
- First user to send /start becomes Super Admin automatically \(bootstrap\)
- Permission check function: canAccess\(userId, sectionId?, moduleId?\) → boolean

### 5. Section \(Department\) Management
- Sections are dynamic containers \(not hardcoded\)
- Super Admin can: create section, edit section name/icon, delete section \(if empty\), enable/disable section, reorder sections
- Each section has: id, name \(Arabic\), nameEn \(English\), icon \(emoji\), isActive, orderIndex
- Sections appear as main menu buttons in the bot
- When user clicks a section → shows list of modules inside it
- Empty section shows ''No modules yet'' message

### 6. Dynamic Module Loader
- At bot startup: scan the modules/ directory
- Each module folder must contain module.config.ts
- Read config and register module in the system
- Module registration includes: which section it belongs to, required permissions, menu label, icon
- If a module''s config is invalid → log warning and skip it \(don''t crash the bot\)
- Provide API for future phases: registerModule\(\), unregisterModule\(\), getModulesBySection\(\)
- In this phase: the loader discovers modules but there are NO actual modules yet \(empty modules/ directory\)

### 7. Maintenance Mode
- Super Admin can toggle maintenance mode ON/OFF via bot command
- When ON: all users except Super Admin see ''Bot is under maintenance, please try later'' in Arabic
- When OFF: normal operation resumes
- Maintenance status stored in Redis \(fast check on every request\)
- Middleware checks maintenance mode before processing any update

### 8. Notification System
- Basic notification service that can send messages to specific users or roles
- Used for: join request notifications to admins, approval/rejection notifications to users, system announcements
- Queue-based using BullMQ \(don''t block the main bot thread\)
- Notification types: SYSTEM, JOIN_REQUEST, APPROVAL, REJECTION, ANNOUNCEMENT
- Store notification history in database for audit

### 9. Audit Logging
- Log every significant user action to database
- Fields: userId, action, targetType, targetId, details \(JSON\), timestamp
- Actions to log: login, role_change, section_create/edit/delete, user_approve/reject, maintenance_toggle, permission_change
- Super Admin can view recent audit logs via bot command
- DO NOT log sensitive data \(passwords, tokens, etc.\)

### 10. Session Management
- Use Redis to store user sessions
- Session contains: userId, role, currentSection, currentModule, currentStep, language preference
- Session expires after 24 hours of inactivity
- On every message: load session from Redis, process, save back to Redis
- Helps with: knowing where the user is in the bot, resuming conversations

## Database Tables needed:
- User \(telegramId, firstName, lastName, phone, role, isActive, language, createdAt, updatedAt\)
- JoinRequest \(userId, name, phone, message, status, reviewedBy, reviewedAt, createdAt\)
- Section \(name, nameEn, icon, isActive, orderIndex, createdAt, updatedAt, createdBy\)
- Module \(name, nameEn, sectionId, icon, isActive, orderIndex, configPath, createdAt\)
- AuditLog \(userId, action, targetType, targetId, details, createdAt\)
- Notification \(userId, type, title, message, isRead, createdAt\)
- AdminScope \(adminUserId, scopeType: ''section'' | ''module'', scopeId, createdAt\)

## What NOT to build in this feature:
- NO Flow Blocks \(that''s Feature 002\)
- NO Flow Engine or Wizard Runner \(that''s Feature 002\)
- NO actual business modules like HR or Finance \(that''s Feature 003+\)
- NO AI features \(that''s Feature 004\)
- The module loader should DISCOVER modules but won''t find any yet
- Section management creates sections but they will be empty \(no modules inside\)

## Key behaviors:
- ALL messages to user must be in Arabic by default
- Bot must handle errors gracefully — never show raw error to user
- Every action must be logged to audit
- Redis must be checked for maintenance mode on every incoming update
- First user gets Super Admin automatically" --number 1 --short-name "platform-core")
